steps.title: Define what a specific example requires
steps.content: The student's goal is to provide a specific example of a slow TV program. A specific example must name a particular program and include concrete identifying details about it. General genre definitions, scholarly commentary, or references to a critic's book do not qualify as a specific example of a program.

steps.title: Test each choice against that requirement
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'British film scholar Helen Wheatley writes about the slow TV genre in her book Spectacular Television: Exploring Televisual Pleasure.': Names a scholar's book, not a specific slow TV program. Fails the requirement entirely. ✗
  • Choice B — 'An example of the slow TV genre can be seen in Nordlandsbanen: Minutt for Minutt, a 2012 Norwegian show featuring an uninterrupted 10-hour real-time broadcast of a train ride from Trondheim to Bodo.': Names the specific program and layers in concrete details — year (2012), country (Norwegian), length (10-hour), and content (train ride from Trondheim to Bodo). ✓
  • Choice C — 'Slow TV programs provide uninterrupted broadcasts of ordinary events, such as train rides, in real time.': Defines the genre in general terms. No specific program is named. ✗
  • Choice D — 'With their uninterrupted broadcasts, slow TV programs offer what film scholar Helen Wheatley calls the "unspectacular spectacle" of ordinary events occurring in real time.': Uses a scholar's quote to characterize the genre. No specific program is identified. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
